creative_brief: Split frame: left side shows a generic supermarket barcode on shrink-wrapped meat, cold and anonymous. Right side shows a hand-written farm name tag on butcher paper (e.g. 'Bundarra Farm, NSW') with warm, natural light. Clean typography overlays the if/then hook.
primary_text:
Every week you buy groceries that quietly erase the farmer who grew them.

No name. No face. No story. Just a barcode and a 2,400km cold chain.

FarmThru exists because that erasure isn't inevitable. It's a choice.

We built a grocery store with zero wholesalers, zero middlemen. Pork from Bundarra Farm. Wild-caught fish from Collins on the South Coast. Eggs from Farmer Brown. Every product traceable to the person who raised it.

No commitment. Order when you want. Collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday.

Own a piece of the grocery store that pays farmers first.
headline: A grocery store that names every farmer
description: Farm-direct. No middlemen. Real names behind every product you collect.
